free_text: Spallation-fission competition in heavy-element
reactions: Th232+He4 and U233+d
Preliminary U233 report data.
Related
Pu239 and Np237 data from Berkeley report.
Berkeley 60-inch cyclotron.
For the thorium foil bombardments the
large amounts of activity produced allowed separate
aliquots of the dissolved target to Be used for the
various fractions analyzed. For the thorium
bombardments the protactinium fraction was purified
by extraction with diisopropyl ketone (dipk) and
elution on an anion-exchange column. The uranium
fraction was purified by an anion-exchange-column
elution. For the U233 bombardments the chemical
separation of the neptunium fraction was described by
vandenbosch. The protactinium fraction was separated
from the neptunium by carrying neptunium (iv)
fluoride on laf3. the counting rates of
alpha-particle-emitting spallation products were
measured by use of a multichannel alpha-pulse-height
analyzer. The counting rates of spallation products
which decay by orbital electron capture were
determined with a methane-flow windowless
proportional counter.
BP
BP: Added REL-REF and Berkeley report.
Spallation product.
A few of the thorium targets were thin uniform deposits
(~500 microg/cm2) of the hydrated thorium or uranium
oxide electrodeposited on aluminum. The amount of
Th232 was determined by alpha counting of the
resulting thin deposits.
The limits of spallation errors are
estimated. The quoted limits of error in the
spallation cross sections do not include
uncertainties in the counting efficiencies used.
For thorium, corrections were applied to account for
the contribution from alpha particle-emitting
daughters of the Th232.
Table I, page 384.

Most of the values for the chain yields are identical
to those for the cumulative yields of the nuclide
given in the other subentry, except that (some) cross
sections were increased by "a small amount" to account
for fission products of the same mass chain not
included experimentally.
The limits fission errors are estimated.
Contribution from Cd115m estimated.

was measured and found negligible.
